---
pagetitle: "Marine Sensitivity Toolkit"
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap-icons@1.11.3/font/bootstrap-icons.min.css">
---

<!-- hero ----------------------------------------------------------------- -->
::::: {.hero}
:::: {.hero-content}

# Marine Sensitivity Toolkit

::: {.tagline}

**The Marine Sensitivity Toolkit**<br>
_atlas of species distributions and federal conservation status in support of U.S. offshore energy planning_

:::

![](images/logo_BOEM.png){.boem-logo}

::::
:::::

<!-- explore the data ----------------------------------------------------- -->
:::: {.section-container}
## Explore the Data

```{=html}
<div class="app-cards">
  <a class="app-card" href="https://app.marinesensitivity.org/mapgl/" target="_blank">
    <i class="bi bi-globe-americas"></i>
    <h3>Composite Scores</h3>
    <p>Interactive map of composite sensitivity scores across Program Areas</p>
  </a>
  <a class="app-card" href="https://app.marinesensitivity.org/mapsp/" target="_blank">
    <i class="bi bi-layers"></i>
    <h3>Species Distributions</h3>
    <p>Individual species distribution models, habitat suitability, and extinction risk</p>
  </a>
  <a class="app-card" href="https://marinesensitivity.org/docs/" target="_blank">
    <i class="bi bi-book"></i>
    <h3>Documentation</h3>
    <p>Technical methods, data sources, and workflow notebooks</p>
  </a>
</div>
```
::::


<!-- methodology ---------------------------------------------------------- -->
:::: {.section-container}
## Methodology Overview

The Marine Sensitivity Toolkit integrates species distribution models, extinction risk scores, and cumulative impact layers to produce composite sensitivity surfaces for offshore energy planning areas. The framework synthesizes data from NOAA, USFWS, IUCN, and academic sources to support evidence-based decision making under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act. For more, see the [Documentation](https://marinesensitivity.org/docs/).

::: {.method-figure}
![Overview of the marine sensitivity methodology](images/overview-methods.svg){.lightbox}
:::
::::

<!-- about ---------------------------------------------------------------- -->
::::: {.section-container}
## About

The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) is mandated under the **Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act** (OCSLA) to balance energy development with environmental protection. The Marine Sensitivity Toolkit provides spatially explicit, species-level sensitivity metrics to inform leasing decisions and environmental reviews across the National OCS Program.

:::: {.about-stats}

::: {.stat-item}
![](images/icons/fish.svg){.stat-icon}

[Fish]{.stat-label}
:::

::: {.stat-item}
![](images/icons/mammal.svg){.stat-icon}

[Marine Mammals]{.stat-label}
:::

::: {.stat-item}
![](images/icons/bird.svg){.stat-icon}

[Seabirds]{.stat-label}
:::

::: {.stat-item}
![](images/icons/productivity.svg){.stat-icon}

[Primary Productivity]{.stat-label}
:::

::: {.stat-item}
<i class="bi bi-three-dots"></i>
[And more]{.stat-label}
:::

::::
:::::

<!-- version history ------------------------------------------------------ -->
::::: {.section-container}
## Version History

:::: {.timeline}

::: {.timeline-item .current}
### v5

[March 2026 – present]{.timeline-date}

Fixed MMPA spatial floor (minimum 20) for FWS-managed marine mammals (walrus, polar bear, sea otter, manatee, dugong) that were missing MMPA protection in earlier versions. Documented the bifurcation of modeling approaches: standard species use a scalar extinction risk weight at scoring time, while sea turtles use a spatially varying extinction risk raster (from SWOT+DPS) multiplied during model merging.
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
### v4

[March 2026]{.timeline-date}

Replaced overly extensive IUCN sea turtle range maps with more realistic State of the World's Turtles (SWOT) global distributions (viewable at [seamap.env.duke.edu/swot](http://seamap.env.duke.edu/swot)). Applied NOAA Distinct Population Segment (DPS) polygons with ESA listing status (NMFS:EN 100, NMFS:TN 50) to differentiate Endangered vs Threatened populations, resulting in more restrictive distributions dominated by ESA risk values. (See workflow [ingest\_turtles-swot-dps](https://marinesensitivity.org/workflows/ingest_turtles-swot-dps.html).)
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
### v3

[February 2026]{.timeline-date}

Program Areas for the National OCS Plan. Ecoregion rescaling. Seven source datasets merged from continuous suitability models (AquaMaps) to binary range maps (NMFS, FWS, BirdLife, IUCN) with masking (where global IUCN range map exists). New species sensitivity weighting based on national ESA or global IUCN extinction risk along with protection from Marine Mammal Protection Act (MMPA) and Migratory Bird Treaty Act (MBTA).
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
### v2

[January 2026]{.timeline-date}

Transition to Program Areas (2026). Individual model layers. Model merging pipeline. Cloud-optimized GeoTIFF storage.
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
### v1

[September 2023 – December 2025]{.timeline-date}

Planning Areas across U.S. EEZ. AquaMaps-based species distributions. Flower plots for multi-taxa visualization. Species tables with conservation status.
:::

::::
:::::
